Up until recently deliveries generally arrived on time and where there has been the odd thing that I've sent back it  worked well, I just took it into the local Post Office and off it went.

However if the package doesn't actually arrive it's a nightmare. Amazon said they had handed it to me, that was a lie, they did not and it wasn't anywhere to be seen, at least near my property and no neighbour came round with it.

This is where it gets really bad. Their 'Customer Service', which consists of useless chatbots and even more useless humans (probably based offshore) just give you the run around, they pass you from one to another, they can't sort anything out at all or even tell you anything useful.

If you battle hard enough, like I did, you'll eventually get a refund, but no explanation at all as to what happened to your package nor any offer of compensation for the trouble experienced.

The thing that really hacked me off was that despite telling them the package never arrived they leave the status at Delivered, Handed to Resident, with the obvious implication that you received it, kept it and are lying about it. Thus they impugn you and I am now considering legal action on that point, it's defamatory.

The other issue is that since they never tell you what happened to your order you're not sure whether it might turn up in a few days, or a week which makes it very difficult to re-order one just in case it turns up the day after.

The people at their head office in London aren't much better, hide behind corporate waffle and seem to me that they are covering up with problems they are having with deliveries.

My advice is don't order unless you want to deal with a nightmare if it doesn't arrive.

In mid-February I purchased a DeLonghi Magnifica Next coffee machine from Amazon.  My trustworthy Nespresso coffee machines were both  on the blink and it was time to invest in a new machine.  My husband seemed to be favouring a bean to cup and although sceptical from my past experience I decided to go along with it.  At a cost of £500 my expectations were high.  The benefits were all there 

Automatic grinding 
High on my list was the integrated Crème latte/cappuccino frothing system,
A great variety of coffee options at the touch  of the screen,
The ability to save your personal favourites,
Automatically filtered water, and
Reminders to carry out maintenance, refill water canister and empty coffee grounds.
 
All in one compact, space saving machine.

and, most importantly, Backed up by Amazon.  What could possibly go wrong.  Obviously no-one wants to buy a product and then find out it is faulty.

Being able to produce a coffee to visitors personal tastes whilst at the same time producing coffee to our individual requirements, a bonus.

My machine was delivered efficiently and as it seemed, in good working order, to my excitement,  and I was anxious to experiment the different coffee options.

However, as with most things these days, there was no user manual.

Looking forward to my cappuccino I set the machine  and pressed the screen setting.  The result - total disappointment.  My instant reaction was that I had obviously made an error but not to worry I will continue to practice.

After a few weeks I came to the decision that  I was maybe doing something wrong and I would just keep practicing until I eventually got it right.  In the meantime I decided to top it up with my Nespresso milk frother.  Eventually I consulted my neighbour who had recommended it, only to be told she had sent her machine back because the frother wasn’t  working!  Alarm bells started ringing - I contacted Amazon as they had sold me the machine!  My machine is approximately 5/6 weeks old.

I contacted Amazon chat to ask about returning it as it was not fit for purpose.  Explained problem to agent and explained could not carry to Royal Mail having arthritis in my hands.  The agent transferred me to a second agent to arrange “pick up“.  

At this  point the chat started to  deteriorate.   In all I was transferred  automatically and without any explanation whatever to 8 different agents, having to repeat myself and hold on until they checked or consulted someone else,  each one giving me differing information,   eventually ok being told I had to take it to Royal  Mail myself and I had to return it to the manufacturer  for “REPAIR”.  Really?!!  I   explained  each time it was sold not fit for purpose and I could not carry it and each time I tripped through to a different agent.  Amazon were not prepared to take any responsibility for product being sold by them not fit for purpose.  If I had purchased elsewhere and not fit for purpose,  it would have been exchanged.  I told them  my contract of purchase was with them and not the manufacturer.  They continued to insist the return period had been exceeded.  Eventually, the last agent cut me off!  Fantastic customer service. 

 I have always had good service in the past and the customer service assistants have always been very helpful and considerate until now.  The first assistant was really helpful and when I explained she said she would pass me through to someone who would arrange collection.    Of course that didn’t happen
.
 I then, in total exasperation, returned to my original order to check the detail, only to find that the information  for return seemed to have been  changed whilst I was on chat to show alternative as a repair.  Apparently, even though product was delivered faulty Amazon will not take any responsibility as it is out of their return period and the only alternative  is the repair o f a brand new just under £500 (expensive) coffee machine.  I was appalled.  Even more so when my neighbour told me how much trouble she had had trying to get the manufacturer to repair hers and that she had had exactly the same problem
 -frother not working.

 I use Amazon a lot for my purchases, and have done for some years, paying for prime delivery for obvious reasons.    However, I will certainly think twice about buying any  more expensive items in the future.  

My advice to Amazon, train your staff to give correct and consistent advice to customers,  whilst not passing them around from pillow to post,  having to repeat the same information over and over again when it’s already there to read. Nor should they  cut customers off - really bad customer service.

  As no manual is provided and the machine was delivered faulty I believe Amazon was under obligation to exchange it.  Who is to say that this is not a product which was returned by another customer as has happened in the past.

Dear Sir/Madam,



I write to you in a formal capacity to seek clear and unequivocal clarification regarding the operational data practices of your Alexa-enabled devices, specifically in relation to the collection, storage, and potential monitoring of in-home audio.



As a provider of consumer technology marketed explicitly for use within private domestic environments, Amazon bears a heightened duty of care and transparency to its customers. Devices such as Alexa are positioned not merely as optional gadgets, but as integrated household systems—frequently operating in close proximity to families, including vulnerable individuals. As such, any ambiguity surrounding their data capture capabilities raises serious concerns.



Accordingly, I require precise answers to the following:



Whether Alexa-enabled devices are continuously listening to ambient conversations, irrespective of wake-word activation.
What technical safeguards are in place to ensure that only intended voice interactions are processed or recorded.
Whether any audio data is stored, reviewed, or accessed by Amazon personnel or third parties, and under what legal or operational framework.
The duration for which any such data is retained and the mechanisms available to consumers for its deletion.
Whether any data collected is used for purposes beyond direct service provision, including but not limited to profiling, targeted advertising, or machine learning training.




I would also remind you that under applicable UK data protection legislation, including the UK GDPR and the Data Protection Act 2018, customers are entitled to full transparency regarding how their personal data—particularly sensitive in-home audio—is processed. Any failure to adequately disclose such practices may constitute a breach of these statutory obligations.



Given the seriousness of this matter, I expect a detailed and comprehensive response within 14 days of receipt of this correspondence. Should your response fail to sufficiently address these concerns, I reserve the right to escalate this matter to the Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO) and to seek independent legal advice.



This is not a speculative enquiry but a legitimate request grounded in consumer rights, privacy expectations, and regulatory compliance. I trust Amazon will treat it with the seriousness it warrants.



Yours faithfully,

I’m a loyal Amazon customer, and frequently order for the website. In October 2025, I purchased a car radio for my Fiat 500. Once received, I installed this into my car. However, after 1 day of use the radio was faulty and overall, it was poor quality. As a result, I returned this unwanted item within the required timeframe and was refunded £119.99. 

In December 2025, I was shocked to see the charge of £119.99 withdraw from my bank again. I contacted customer service multiple times to request a refund. At first, I was told
no return was received as “I did not send the radio back” I have to pay the full amount. 

After exhausting the use of their customer service, I had no choice but to contact my credit card provider for help, as there was no way I would pay for an item I do not have and returned. ZXHQ 2026 Diary A5 Page a Day, Jan-Dec.

Regrettably, I find myself with a life fuller than I'd ever imagined—though not in the way we dream about. My calendar tells a story I never wanted to write, filled with medical appointments and clinical trials for cancer treatments that consume my days.

You see, a diary demands what I can no longer easily give. It requires two hands when one is often occupied by my phone, and even a simple spiral-bound book has become a challenge I'd rather avoid. Stomach cancer brings its own unwelcome schedule of frequent bathroom visits, and inevitably, someone will call right when I'm least prepared to answer.

My germaphobic son certainly won't be offering assistance, in the bathroom! I crave whatever privacy remains as my health continues its unfortunate decline. As much as I love the idea of a proper diary—that nostalgic companion of generations past—the simple act of turning pages and writing has become surprisingly difficult with hands that no longer cooperate as they once did.

What I actually need is modest: just the date, time, location, and a few brief notes. My medical team knows what they're doing; detailed records aren't necessary when professionals are guiding my care. A wall calendar serves my purpose far better than an elaborate diary with all those extras—day-per-page layouts, religious information, expense trackers, habit monitors, inspirational quotes, maps, and time zones. They're lovely touches, truly, but excessive for what I require.

Yes, a portable diary would be wonderful in the hospital where medical staff could add their own notes, but the thought of losing it and needing a duplicate troubles me deeply. 

So I hope to gift this beautiful diary to someone who will treasure it as it deserves. Word travels quickly about such wonderful presents, especially when they arrive with a matching pen—sea green with sea green, purple with purple. A spiral-bound option would have been ideal, but what I really covet is a dictaphone.

For now, I lumber back from my throne to the coffee table whenever the phone rings, the zimmer frame clanking loudly, hoping it's not another nuisance caller. Anyone who's survived this particular journey uses far different describing words for that awkward shuffle than I can print here.

In mid-February I purchased a DeLonghi Magnifica Next coffee machine from Amazon.  My trustworthy Nespresso coffee machines were both  on the blink and it was time to invest in a new machine.  My husband seemed to be favouring a bean to cup and although sceptical from my past experience I decided to go along with it.  At a cost of £500 my expectations were high.  The benefits were all there 

Automatic grinding 
High on my list was the integrated Crème latte/cappuccino frothing system,
A great variety of coffee options at the touch  of the screen,
The ability to save your personal favourites,
Automatically filtered water, and
Reminders to carry out maintenance, refill water canister and empty coffee grounds.
 
All in one compact, space saving machine.

and, most importantly, Backed up by Amazon.  What could possibly go wrong.  Obviously no-one wants to buy a product and then find out it is faulty.

Being able to produce a coffee to visitors personal tastes whilst at the same time producing coffee to our individual requirements, a bonus.

My machine was delivered efficiently and as it seemed, in good working order, to my excitement,  and I was anxious to experiment the different coffee options.

However, as with most things these days, there was no user manual.

Looking forward to my cappuccino I set the machine  and pressed the screen setting.  The result - total disappointment.  My instant reaction was that I had obviously made an error but not to worry I will continue to practice.

After a few weeks I came to the decision that  I was maybe doing something wrong and I would just keep practicing until I eventually got it right.  In the meantime I decided to top it up with my Nespresso milk frother.  Eventually I consulted my neighbour who had recommended it, only to be told she had sent her machine back because the frother wasn’t  working!  Alarm bells started ringing - I contacted Amazon as they had sold me the machine!  My machine is approximately 5/6 weeks old.

I contacted Amazon chat to ask about returning it as it was not fit for purpose.  Explained problem to agent and explained could not carry to Royal Mail having arthritis in my hands.  The agent transferred me to a second agent to arrange “pick up“.  

At this  point the chat started to  deteriorate.   In all I was transferred  automatically and without any explanation whatever to 8 different agents, having to repeat myself and hold on until they checked or consulted someone else,  each one giving me differing information,   eventually ok being told I had to take it to Royal  Mail myself and I had to return it to the manufacturer  for “REPAIR”.  Really?!!  I   explained  each time it was sold not fit for purpose and I could not carry it and each time I tripped through to a different agent.  Amazon were not prepared to take any responsibility for product being sold by them not fit for purpose.  If I had purchased elsewhere and not fit for purpose,  it would have been exchanged.  I told them  my contract of purchase was with them and not the manufacturer.  They continued to insist the return period had been exceeded.  Eventually, the last agent cut me off!  Fantastic customer service. 

 I have always had good service in the past and the customer service assistants have always been very helpful and considerate until now.  The first assistant was really helpful and when I explained she said she would pass me through to someone who would arrange collection.    Of course that didn’t happen
.
 I then, in total exasperation, returned to my original order to check the detail, only to find that the information  for return seemed to have been  changed whilst I was on chat to show alternative as a repair.  Apparently, even though product was delivered faulty Amazon will not take any responsibility as it is out of their return period and the only alternative  is the repair o f a brand new just under £500 (expensive) coffee machine.  I was appalled.  Even more so when my neighbour told me how much trouble she had had trying to get the manufacturer to repair hers and that she had had exactly the same problem
 -frother not working.

 I use Amazon a lot for my purchases, and have done for some years, paying for prime delivery for obvious reasons.    However, I will certainly think twice about buying any  more expensive items in the future.  

My advice to Amazon, train your staff to give correct and consistent advice to customers,  whilst not passing them around from pillow to post,  having to repeat the same information over and over again when it’s already there to read. Nor should they  cut customers off - really bad customer service.

  As no manual is provided and the machine was delivered faulty I believe Amazon was under obligation to exchange it.  Who is to say that this is not a product which was returned by another customer as has happened in the past.

I had an extremely disappointing and frustrating experience with SHEIN that I feel compelled to share.

I placed an order on march 15 for essential pregnancy items, expecting delivery within the stated timeframe (April 7–12). However, from the beginning, the process was filled with delays, lack of transparency, and unprofessional customer service.

The tracking showed minimal updates, and when I contacted support multiple times, I received completely contradictory information. Some agents told me the package had already arrived in Lebanon, while the shipping company iMile confirmed it was still in China. Later, I was told it had shipped, then again something different. There was clearly no consistency or access to accurate logistics data.

Despite explaining that this order was urgent due to my wife’s due date, the responses I received were generic, dismissive, and at times rude. Instead of taking responsibility, I was even told to contact the shipping company myself to cancel the order—something that is clearly not the customer’s responsibility when ordering through SHEIN.

After pushing repeatedly, I was informed that the shipment would be intercepted and refunded only after return, adding even more delay and uncertainty. Meanwhile, tracking eventually showed customs clearance activity (even inconsistently referencing Riyadh), but since April 8 there have been no updates at all.

At this point (April 17), the package is effectively untraceable, customer service remains unhelpful, and I have completely lost confidence in both the delivery process and the support system.

Key issues:
	•	No tracking updates for long periods
	•	Conflicting and inaccurate information from support agents
	•	Lack of accountability
	•	No urgency handling despite clear time-sensitive needs
	•	Poor communication and unprofessional responses

This experience has been stressful and unacceptable, especially given the importance of the items ordered. I would strongly caution others in Lebanon (or similar regions) against relying on SHEIN for time-sensitive purchases.

I expected basic reliability and transparency, but unfortunately received neither.
